citation.sourceDocument: Rhode Island Division of Taxation Advisory ADV 2025-22 (2026 inflation-adjusted PIT brackets, standard deduction, exemption; R.I. Gen. Laws 44-30-2.6)
citation.sourceNote: Rhode Island's three rates — 3.75% / 4.75% / 5.99% — are identical for every filing status (R.I. Gen. Laws 44-30-2.6(c)): the bracket thresholds do NOT vary by status, only the standard deduction and personal exemption do. For 2026 the rates apply at 4.75% over $82,050 and 5.99% over $186,450 (the same dollar breakpoints for single, joint, and head of household). Rhode Island stacks both a standard deduction ($11,200 single / $22,400 married jointly / $16,800 head of household) AND a personal exemption ($5,250 per taxpayer — $5,250 single and head of household, $10,500 married jointly) before the brackets — the Virginia/Kansas stack, now on a uniform schedule. Every figure is the 2026 inflation-adjusted amount from the Rhode Island Division of Taxation's Advisory ADV 2025-22 (issued 2025-11-03); the base tax amounts were verified to the penny against the advisory (3.75% of $82,050 = $3,076.88; plus 4.75% of the next $104,400 = $8,035.88 at the top breakpoint). Rhode Island phases out the standard deduction and exemptions for modified AGI between $261,000 and $290,800 (2026), and the $5,250-per-dependent dependency exemption — both omitted at launch fidelity (the no-dependent, below-phase-out filer, like every seeded state), so the figure errs slightly high for very high earners — the conservative side. Cross-checked against the Tax Foundation 2026 table; verified against the ADV 2025-22 PDF on 2026-06-06.
